subsection.name: Description
subsection.div: Checks the padding of the body of type definitions (classes, interfaces, enums, records). That is, whether a blank line is required immediately after the opening brace and/or immediately before the closing brace of type bodies. Empty type bodies are exempt from this check by default.
subsection.p: A blank line (padding) line is a line containing only whitespace characters.

tr.td: Allow empty type bodies to omit blank line padding.
Require a blank line before the type body closing brace.
Require a blank line after the type body opening brace.
Control whether to skip checking of inner types.
Control whether to skip checking of local types.

subset of tokens
CLASS_DEF , INTERFACE_DEF , ENUM_DEF , RECORD_DEF , ANNOTATION_DEF .

subsection.name: Examples
subsection.p: To configure the check to require blank lines even for empty type bodies:
To configure the check to not require a blank line after the opening brace:
To configure the check to not require a blank line before the closing brace:
To configure the check to require blank lines for inner classes too:
To configure the check to require blank lines for local classes too:
To configure the check to only run on interfaces, enums, annotations, and records (bypassing classes):

pre.code: <module name="Checker"> <module name="TreeWalker"> <module name="TypeBodyPadding"> <property name="tokens" value="INTERFACE_DEF, RECORD_DEF, ENUM_DEF, ANNOTATION_DEF"/> </module> </module> </module>

subsection.name: Violation Messages
subsection.p: All messages can be customized if the default message doesn't suit you. Please see the documentation to learn how to.

subsection.name: Fully Qualified Name
subsection.p: Use this fully qualified class name in configuration when an exact class reference is required.
